memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents — A Yankee in the Trenches by R. Derby Holmes'

memes.label: Army Service Corps
memes.description: "The Army Service Corps — the British Army's logistics and supply branch, responsible for transporting food, ammunition, and equipment to the front; mentioned in Holmes's memoir through the soldiers' sardonic humor about the quality of its bread."

memes.label: Artillery
memes.description: Artillery in World War I trench warfare, as experienced by R. Derby Holmes in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Barbed Wire
memes.description: Barbed wire — the defensive entanglement that defined the landscape of No Man's Land in World War I, as encountered by R. Derby Holmes in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Barrage
memes.description: Artillery barrage tactics in World War I—the sustained bombardment preceding and supporting infantry attacks, as described in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Battalion
memes.description: "The fundamental tactical unit of the British infantry in World War I — roughly 1,000 men organized into four companies, the battalion was the soldier's military family, the largest unit with which he identified personally, and the administrative backbone of trench warfare."

memes.label: Battle
memes.description: "The set-piece military engagement that defined World War I — from the vast offensives of the Somme to the intimate, personal fight for survival in a captured trench at High Wood, as recorded by an American volunteer in the British Army."

memes.label: Battle of High Wood
memes.description: The Battle of High Wood — a fierce engagement on the Somme battlefield in the summer of 1916, where R. Derby Holmes fought alongside the first tanks and where his war ultimately ended.

memes.label: Battle of the Somme
memes.description: "The Battle of the Somme (1916) — one of the largest and bloodiest battles of World War I, in which R. Derby Holmes fought and was wounded."

memes.label: Bayonet
memes.description: "The blade fixed to the muzzle of the infantry rifle — the weapon of last resort, the instrument of the trench raid, and the symbol of cold steel in an age of industrialized warfare."

memes.label: Bellinger
memes.description: Bellinger — Holmes's patrol companion during a tense night reconnaissance near the German lines, whose caution during the escape saved both men.

memes.label: Big Push
memes.description: The Big Push — the massive Allied offensive on the Somme in the summer of 1916, the central military operation of Holmes' wartime experience.

memes.label: Billet
memes.description: "Billets — the lodgings behind the lines where soldiers were quartered during rest periods, a world apart from the trenches and a vital respite from the front."

memes.label: Billets
memes.description: Billets — the quarters where soldiers rested and recuperated behind the front lines, ranging from pigpens to palaces.

memes.label: Blighty
memes.description: "Blighty — the British soldier's affectionate term for England and home, the place every Tommy longed to return to, whether on leave, through a 'Blighty wound,' or at war's end."

memes.label: Blighty Wound
memes.description: "A Blighty wound — a battlefield injury serious enough to warrant evacuation to England, the darkly coveted 'ticket home' for the front-line soldier."

memes.label: Boche
memes.description: "Boche — the most common British trench slang term for a German soldier, a word that embodied both contempt and grudging respect, used throughout the war by Tommy to refer to the enemy across no man's land."

memes.label: Bombing Party
memes.description: "A hand-picked team of soldiers specializing in close-quarters grenade attacks — the trench warfare equivalent of shock troops, tasked with clearing enemy positions under cover of darkness."

memes.label: Bones
memes.description: Bones — an educated British soldier in Holmes's unit, a philosophical reader who shared a shell-hole refuge with Holmes and mused on the nature of war.

memes.label: Boston
memes.description: Boston, Massachusetts, was Holmes's hometown and the American city where he enlisted for service before sailing to England.

memes.label: British Army
memes.description: "The British Army in World War I — the military organization that fielded millions of men on the Western Front, including the American volunteer R. Derby Holmes."

memes.label: Bully Beef
memes.description: Bully beef — the ubiquitous tinned corned beef that formed the backbone of the British Tommy's diet in the trenches of World War I, as recorded by R. Derby Holmes in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Canchy
memes.description: Canchy was a French village behind the lines where Holmes's brigade spent ten days under heavy shellfire — a so-called "rest" that proved anything but restful.

memes.label: Captain Green
memes.description: Captain Green — company commander in Holmes's regiment who ordered the cover-up of a German atrocity, promoted Holmes to bombing duty, and was mortally wounded on the Somme.

memes.label: Casualty
memes.description: Casualties in trench warfare — the killed, wounded, and missing, the constant toll that shaped every aspect of a soldier's experience on the Western Front.

memes.label: Char
memes.description: "Char — the British Tommy's universal word for tea, the dark, strong, often-overbrewed beverage that was the soldier's primary comfort, social lubricant, and morning ritual in the trenches of the Western Front."

memes.label: Chat
memes.description: "Chat — British trench slang for the body louse; a constant and maddening companion of every soldier in the trenches, whose presence turned even moments of rest into torment."

memes.label: Chat
memes.description: "Chat" was British trench slang for body lice — the near-universal vermin infestation that plagued soldiers in the trenches.

memes.label: Click
memes.description: "To click" or "click it" was British trench slang meaning to be killed or hit — an understated euphemism for death.

memes.label: Communication Trench
memes.description: Communication trenches in World War I — the long, winding passages connecting front-line, support, and reserve trenches, as described by R. Derby Holmes in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Concepts by Era
memes.description: Index of all concept pages from A Yankee in the Trenches, grouped by historical era

memes.label: Cootie
memes.description: "Cootie — the Tommy's name for the body louse, the universal and relentless parasite of the trenches that tormented every soldier on the Western Front, driving men to desperate measures in the ritual known as the 'cootie carnival.'"

memes.label: Shell Crater
memes.description: Bowl-shaped depression in the ground caused by the explosion of an artillery shell or underground mine, a ubiquitous terrain feature of the WWI Western Front.

memes.label: Daily Life in the Trenches
memes.description: "An analytical synthesis of daily life in the Western Front trenches as recorded in R. Derby Holmes's memoir 'A Yankee in the Trenches' — from the rhythms of stand-to and fatigue duty to the small comforts of char, fags, and rum that sustained the British Tommy through months of mud and danger."

memes.label: Divisional Follies
memes.description: "A vaudeville entertainment troupe formed within a British division on the Western Front — ten former actors detailed to amuse the troops, performing shows behind the lines and raising funds for the Red Cross."

memes.label: Dixie
memes.description: An oblong iron cooking pot used with British Army field kitchens in World War I, as described by R. Derby Holmes in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Dover
memes.description: Dover, the English Channel port and training site, was where Holmes and other recruits received their initial military instruction before deployment to France.

memes.label: Dressing Station
memes.description: "The forward-most medical post in the British Army's casualty evacuation chain — a tent or dugout close behind the front line where wounded soldiers received their first professional medical attention and the critical decision of whether they would return to duty or be sent rearward."

memes.label: Duck Walk
memes.description: A slatted wooden walkway built across soft ground in the trenches of World War I, essential for movement through the mud of the Western Front

memes.label: Dug-out
memes.description: "Dug-outs — the underground shelters carved into trench walls, offering soldiers their only respite from shellfire and the elements."

memes.label: England
memes.description: England, affectionately called "Blighty" by British soldiers, served as the home front, training ground, and recovery destination for wounded troops during WWI.

memes.label: Equipment
memes.description: The personal military gear and accoutrements carried by British soldiers in the trenches of WWI, typically weighing over seventy pounds.

memes.label: Estaminet
memes.description: Small French bar or cafe in billet towns where WWI soldiers spent their leisure time drinking, gambling, and socializing.

memes.label: Events Index
memes.description: Index of battles, raids, patrols, and other military events recounted in A Yankee in the Trenches

memes.label: Fag
memes.description: "Fag — British military slang for a cigarette; the universal comfort and currency of the trenches, where Tommy smoked to steady his nerves, pass the time, and find a moment's pleasure amid the horror."

memes.label: Fatigue Duty
memes.description: Manual labour assignments given to soldiers behind the lines, including road mending, trench repair, and supply carrying — often used as both routine duty and informal punishment.

memes.label: Fire Step
memes.description: The fire step — the raised platform inside a World War I trench from which soldiers fired their rifles and observed No Man's Land, as experienced by R. Derby Holmes in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: First Aid
memes.description: First aid in the trenches — the emergency medical care available to wounded soldiers on the Western Front, from field dressings to dressing stations.

memes.label: France
memes.description: "France — the country that was the principal theatre of the Western Front, where millions of British, French, and German soldiers fought and died, and where an American volunteer named R. Derby Holmes experienced the full horror and occasional strange beauty of the Great War."

memes.label: Fritz
memes.description: "Fritz" was the most common British trench nickname for German soldiers, used with a mixture of familiarity, contempt, and grudging respect.

memes.label: Funk Hole
memes.description: A small dug-out shelter in the side of a trench, providing soldiers with rudimentary protection from shellfire and a place to rest in World War I

memes.label: Gas Helmet
memes.description: A protective respirator issued to soldiers in World War I to guard against poison gas attacks, a vital but uncomfortable piece of trench equipment

memes.label: Gas Mask
memes.description: The gas mask — called "gas helmet" by British soldiers — was the essential piece of protective equipment against poison gas, carried by every soldier at all times in the trenches.

memes.label: Gas Warfare
memes.description: "Chemical warfare on the Western Front — the use of poison and tear gas, and the primitive protective equipment soldiers relied upon to survive gas attacks."

memes.label: Poison Gas
memes.description: Poison gas was one of the most feared weapons of the First World War — a silent, invisible killer that transformed the battlefield and required soldiers to carry specialized protective equipment at all times.

memes.label: Germans
memes.description: "The German soldier of the Western Front as seen through the eyes of his British adversary — known variously as Fritz, Jerry, the Boche, and the Hun, a complex figure who was simultaneously a feared enemy, a mirror image, and occasionally an object of grudging respect."

memes.label: Going Over the Top
memes.description: "Going over the top" — the act of climbing out of the trench and advancing across No Man's Land to attack the enemy.

memes.label: Got It
memes.description: "Got it" was British trench slang meaning to be hit or killed — the most direct and fatalistic of the Tommy's death euphemisms.

memes.label: Hand Grenade
memes.description: The hand grenade — particularly the Mills bomb — revolutionized trench warfare, giving individual soldiers the ability to clear dugouts and trench bays with explosive force.

memes.label: Bully-Grenay
memes.description: Bully-Grenay — a small French village behind the lines near Lens, where Holmes and his battalion rested for two days after a trench raid, a rare moment of relative safety and comfort.

memes.label: Grouse Spots
memes.description: Grouse Spots — a section of the British front-line trenches near Vimy Ridge where R. Derby Holmes conducted solo night patrols into No Man's Land.

memes.label: Le Havre
memes.description: Le Havre, the major French Channel port, was the primary entry point for British and Commonwealth troops arriving in France during WWI.

memes.label: Steel Helmet
memes.description: The steel helmet — called "tin hat" by the Tommies — was the most essential piece of protective equipment issued to British soldiers, dramatically reducing head wounds from shrapnel and shell fragments.

memes.label: High Wood
memes.description: "High Wood — a dense woodland on the Somme battlefield that became the scene of one of the most intense and costly engagements of 1916, and the place where R. Derby Holmes's war reached its violent climax."

memes.label: Hospital
memes.description: Medical facilities where wounded and sick soldiers were treated, ranging from frontline aid posts to base hospitals in France and England.

memes.label: Hun
memes.description: "Hun" was a derogatory British and Allied term for German soldiers during WWI, originating from Kaiser Wilhelm II's 1900 speech comparing German troops to Attila's Huns.

memes.label: Iron Ration
memes.description: The iron ration — the emergency food reserve carried by British soldiers in World War I, consisting of bully beef and biscuits meant only for dire necessity, as described by R. Derby Holmes in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Kip
memes.description: "Kip — British military slang for sleep or a nap; one of the most cherished activities in the exhausted world of the trenches, where every moment of rest was precious."

memes.label: Knuckle Knife
memes.description: The knuckle knife was a short dagger with a weighted knuckle-duster grip, designed for silent close-quarters combat during trench raids.

memes.label: Leave
memes.description: Authorized absence from military duty, ranging from embarkation leave before deployment to France, to short furloughs and rare home leave during active service.

memes.label: Leicester Regiment
memes.description: "A British Army infantry regiment whose soldiers fought alongside Holmes's unit during the Somme offensive — personified in the memoir by Private Crane, a young soldier acting as corporal who confronted a German officer at bayonet-point."

memes.label: Lens
memes.description: Lens — a French mining town in the Pas-de-Calais region near the Western Front, opposite Bully-Grenay and part of the operational area where Holmes served.

memes.label: Lieutenant Blofeld
memes.description: "Lieutenant Blofeld — the platoon commander who organized the battalion's first trench raid, personally led his men over the top, and represented the ideal of the democratic British front-line officer."

memes.label: Lieutenant May
memes.description: Lieutenant May, a British officer who led Holmes and his comrades on the night bombing raid near High Wood, described as "one of the bravest men I ever knew."

memes.label: Listening Post
memes.description: A forward position in no man's land where soldiers listened for enemy movements at night, a vital reconnaissance practice in WWI trench warfare

memes.label: London
memes.description: London, the capital of England and the British Empire, served as the arrival point, training hub, and hospital destination for soldiers during WWI.

memes.label: Machine Gun
memes.description: "The machine gun — the weapon that more than any other defined the killing power of World War I, turning no man's land into a death zone and making the massed infantry assault a suicidal proposition against prepared positions."

memes.label: Mad Harry
memes.description: "Mad Harry — a British lieutenant whose reckless courage and eccentric behavior earned him his nickname, and whose death on the battlefield was as strange as his life."

memes.label: Mametz
memes.description: Mametz — a village on the Somme battlefield, captured by British forces during the early days of the 1916 offensive, part of the operational area where Holmes later served.

memes.label: Marching
memes.description: "Route marching — the endless foot-slogging that moved armies across France, a defining physical ordeal of the soldier's experience before and between battles."

memes.label: Mess Tin
memes.description: The soldier's mess tin — a versatile metal container used as teapot, frying pan, and plate in the trenches.

memes.label: Mills Bomb
memes.description: The standard British hand grenade of WWI, a serrated cast-iron fragmentation grenade widely used in trench raids and close combat.

memes.label: Mine Explosion
memes.description: Underground mine warfare on the Western Front, as experienced by the author at Vimy Ridge, where sappers tunneled beneath No Man's Land to plant explosives under enemy positions.

memes.label: Minnies
memes.description: British soldier slang for German trench mortar shells fired from the Minenwerfer, a short-range high-trajectory weapon much feared in the trenches.

memes.label: Mr. May
memes.description: Lieutenant May — a British officer described by Holmes as "one of the bravest men I ever knew," who commanded a bombing party raid on a German trench.

memes.label: Nap
memes.description: "Nap" or "napper" was British trench slang for the head — most often heard in the urgent warning "keep your napper down!" to avoid sniper fire.

memes.label: Night Patrol
memes.description: Night patrol operations in No Man's Land — the dangerous work of scouting enemy positions, gathering intelligence, and probing German lines under cover of darkness, as experienced by the author at Vimy Ridge and the Grouse Spots.

memes.label: No Man's Land
memes.description: "No man's land — the strip of ground between opposing trenches, a dead zone of shell craters, barbed wire, and unburied dead that defined the geography of the Western Front."

memes.label: Organizations Directory
memes.description: Directory of military units, battalions, regiments, and organizations referenced in A Yankee in the Trenches

memes.label: Over the Top
memes.description: "Going 'over the top' — the moment soldiers climbed out of their trenches to advance across no man's land into enemy fire, the defining act of courage and terror on the Western Front."

memes.label: Parapet
memes.description: The parapet—the forward wall of a trench—as a defining feature of World War I trench warfare, described in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Passchendaele
memes.description: "The Battle of Passchendaele (1917) — the Third Battle of Ypres, infamous for its mud and catastrophic casualties, in which Holmes fought."

memes.label: Patrol
memes.description: "Night patrol — the dangerous work of scouting no man's land in the darkness, gathering intelligence and probing enemy positions between the trench lines."

memes.label: Trench Periscope
memes.description: Optical observation device used in WWI trenches to view No Man's Land without exposing the observer to enemy fire.

memes.label: Personnel Index
memes.description: Complete index of all people mentioned in A Yankee in the Trenches — soldiers, officers, family members, and historical figures

memes.label: Persuader
memes.description: The "persuader" was a short, heavy bludgeon or trench club used by British raiding parties for silent close-quarters killing during night trench raids.

memes.label: Pip-Squeak
memes.description: The "pip-squeak" — a small-caliber German artillery shell named by British soldiers for its distinctive two-part sound — described in R. Derby Holmes's "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Pistol
memes.description: The pistol — the officer's sidearm carried in the trenches, used for close-quarters combat, patrol work, and as a last line of personal defense.

memes.label: Places Directory
memes.description: Directory of all locations mentioned in A Yankee in the Trenches — battlefields, trench sectors, towns, and countries

memes.label: Platoon
memes.description: "The smallest independent tactical unit of the British infantry — roughly fifty men under a lieutenant, the platoon was the soldier's immediate military family, the unit that ate together, fought together, and died together in the trenches of the Western Front."

memes.label: Queen's Royal West Surrey Regiment
memes.description: "The Queen's Royal West Surrey Regiment — the British infantry regiment in which R. Derby Holmes served, one of the oldest regiments in the British Army."

memes.label: R. Derby Holmes
memes.description: R. Derby Holmes, the American author and narrator of "A Yankee in the Trenches," a Bostonian who enlisted in the British Army during WWI.

memes.label: Raid
memes.description: "The trench raid — a stealth infiltration of enemy lines by a small party of volunteers, armed with bombs and bayonets, designed to capture prisoners, gather intelligence, and maintain the offensive spirit of the unit between major battles."

memes.label: Raiding Party
memes.description: A small force of soldiers assembled for a surprise attack on enemy trenches, a key tactic of WWI trench warfare described by R. Derby Holmes

memes.label: Ration Party
memes.description: A party of soldiers detailed to carry food and supplies from the rear to the front-line trenches, a dangerous nightly duty in World War I

memes.label: Red Cross
memes.description: "The International Red Cross — the humanitarian organization providing medical care and relief to wounded soldiers during World War I, appearing in Holmes's memoir as the operator of hospital trains, ambulances, and recipient of charitable funds."

memes.label: Revolver
memes.description: The revolver was the standard sidearm of British officers and trench raiders — a close-range backup weapon for the chaos of trench combat.

memes.label: Rifle
memes.description: "The standard-issue infantry firearm of World War I — the soldier's constant companion, tool of combat, and object of obsessive maintenance in the mud of the trenches."

memes.label: Rouen
memes.description: Rouen, a major French city on the Seine, served as a key transit point and hospital center for British and Commonwealth forces during WWI.

memes.label: Royal Fusiliers
memes.description: "The Royal Fusiliers — a British Army infantry regiment that appears in Holmes's memoir, one of the units operating alongside his own."

memes.label: Rum Ration
memes.description: The daily rum ration — a vital morale booster and ritual of trench life, issued to British soldiers on the Western Front.

memes.label: Sandbag
memes.description: Sandbags as the essential building material of World War I trench fortifications, as described in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Sanford
memes.description: "Sanford — a fellow soldier and friend of Holmes, an American volunteer whose fate in the trenches is recorded with poignant brevity."

memes.label: Sap
memes.description: The sap — a trench extension dug toward enemy lines for mining, reconnaissance, or approach purposes in World War I, as referenced by R. Derby Holmes in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Sap Head
memes.description: A sap head — the forward terminus of a sap trench, dug toward enemy lines for listening posts, mining operations, or as a jumping-off point for raids.

memes.label: Sergeant Major
memes.description: The unnamed Sergeant Major — a recurring NCO figure in Holmes's battalion who greeted new arrivals, issued rum, and was killed alongside Captain Green in the final offensive.

memes.label: Sergeant Page
memes.description: Sergeant Page — a British NCO in Holmes's company who assigned him to ration party duty and was later sent to Havre as an instructor.

memes.label: Shell
memes.description: Artillery shells in World War I trench warfare — the projectiles that defined the daily threat of life on the Western Front, as experienced by R. Derby Holmes

memes.label: Shell Shock
memes.description: "Shell shock — the psychological trauma of trench warfare, a condition poorly understood at the time yet devastating in its effects on soldiers."

memes.label: Shrapnel
memes.description: "Shrapnel — the artillery projectile designed to burst in the air and rain steel balls down on exposed troops, the weapon that caused the majority of casualties on the Western Front and the one that ended R. Derby Holmes's war at High Wood."

memes.label: Trench Slang Glossary
memes.description: Glossary of military slang, trench talk, and World War I colloquialisms from A Yankee in the Trenches

memes.label: Sniper
memes.description: "Snipers — the hidden marksmen who made any exposure above the parapet a potentially fatal mistake, a constant psychological presence on the Western Front."

memes.label: Somme
memes.description: The Somme river and sector in northern France, site of major WWI battles and the setting for much of the author's trench warfare experience.

memes.label: Souchez
memes.description: Souchez — the river and sector on the Western Front where Holmes's battalion held trench positions, part of the Vimy Ridge area of operations.

memes.label: Souvenir
memes.description: "Souvenir hunting" — collecting German equipment and trophies from the battlefield — was a widespread obsession among British soldiers, despite being dangerous and officially discouraged.

memes.label: Stokes Mortar
memes.description: The Stokes mortar was a British 3-inch trench mortar that fired high-explosive bombs at a high angle, providing infantry units with portable, close-range artillery support.

memes.label: Stretcher Bearer
memes.description: Soldiers assigned to carry wounded men from the battlefield to aid stations, one of the most dangerous non-combat roles in World War I

memes.label: Tank
memes.description: "The tank — a revolutionary armored fighting vehicle that made its combat debut on the Somme in 1916, a terrifying new weapon that crawled across no man's land, crushed barbed wire, and struck fear into the German defenders who first encountered it."

memes.label: Tin Hat
memes.description: The "tin hat" — British soldier slang for the steel helmet — essential protective equipment against shell fragments and shrapnel in World War I trench warfare

memes.label: Tommy
memes.description: "The archetypal British soldier of World War I — known universally as 'Tommy' or 'Tommy Atkins,' the resilient, grumbling, irrepressible infantryman who endured the trenches with dark humor and stoic courage."

memes.label: Traverse
memes.description: The traverse—angled bends in trench lines that limited blast and bullet travel—as a critical defensive feature of World War I trenches, described in "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Trench
memes.description: "The trench system of World War I — the defining environment of the Western Front, shaping every aspect of a soldier's daily existence from combat to survival."

memes.label: Trench Discipline
memes.description: "The system of rules and punishments governing soldier behavior in the trenches, from field punishment to the informal discipline of the front-line unit."

memes.label: Trench Fatigue
memes.description: "Fatigue duty — the exhausting manual labor assigned to soldiers between combat operations, from carrying supplies to repairing trenches under fire."

memes.label: Trench Life
memes.description: Daily life in the trenches of the Western Front — the routines, hardships, and small comforts that defined a soldier's existence between battles.

memes.label: Trench Raid
memes.description: "Trench raids — stealth infiltrations of enemy lines to capture prisoners, gather intelligence, and inflict damage, among the most harrowing operations of the Western Front."

memes.label: Trench Rations
memes.description: "Trench rations — the food that sustained soldiers on the Western Front, centered on bully beef and biscuits, supplemented by tea and the occasional luxury parcel."

memes.label: Uniform
memes.description: The standard military dress worn by British soldiers in WWI, consisting of khaki service dress, puttees, and various regimental distinctions.

memes.label: Very Light
memes.description: An illumination flare fired from a signal pistol, used extensively in World War I trench warfare for night visibility and target illumination

memes.label: Vimy Ridge
memes.description: Vimy Ridge, a strategic escarpment in northern France, was the scene of fierce fighting and a prominent terrain feature on the Western Front.

memes.label: Vimy Ridge
memes.description: "The Battle of Vimy Ridge (April 1917) — a Canadian Corps victory in which Holmes participated, remembered as a defining moment for Canadian national identity."

memes.label: Water Bottle
memes.description: A standard-issue water canteen carried by British infantry soldiers in World War I, part of the eighty-pound kit each man bore into the trenches

memes.label: Weapons and Equipment Index
memes.description: Complete index of weapons, military equipment, and armaments mentioned in A Yankee in the Trenches

memes.label: Wellsie
memes.description: Wellsie — a cheerful cockney soldier in Holmes's company who volunteered him for ration party duty and was a steady companion through the war.

memes.label: Whiz-Bang
memes.description: The "whiz-bang" — a small-caliber German artillery shell named by British soldiers for its distinctive sound — described in R. Derby Holmes's "A Yankee in the Trenches"

memes.label: Windy
memes.description: "Windy" or having the "wind up" was British trench slang for being nervous, jumpy, or frightened — a universally understood condition in the trenches.

memes.label: Y.M.C.A.
memes.description: "The Young Men's Christian Association — one of the most significant welfare organizations on the Western Front, operating warm, dry huts behind the lines where soldiers could read, write letters, hear music, and find respite from the trenches."

memes.label: Zero Hour
memes.description: The "zero hour" military timing convention used on the Western Front — the fixed time for any operation, from which all actions were scheduled as plus or minus zero
